messages.text:
According to the context, a positional encoder is used to inject information about the relative or absolute position of the tokens in a sequence. This is necessary because the model contains no recurrence and no convolution, and therefore needs a way to make use of the order of the sequence.

The positional encodings are added to the input embeddings, allowing the model to understand the position of each token in the sequence. This is done to prevent the model from treating the input sequence as a set of unordered elements, and to enable it to capture the relationships between tokens based on their position in the sequence.

In other words, the positional encoder helps the model to understand that the order of the tokens matters, and that the same token can have different meanings depending on its position in the sequence.
